Defendant Judith Brass' Motion To Compel Production Of Medical Records From Dr. Friedlander And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-20-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. DARRELL V. NGUYEN, Esq. of Tyson & Mendes LLP (Tel: 628-253-5070) for Defendant/Cross-Complainant Judith M. Brass, an Individual and Trustee of the Judith M. Brass Revocable Trust dated September 25, 2012;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: Defendant Judith Brass' Motion To Compel Production Of Medical Records From Dr. Friedlander And Request For Sanctions.

Defendant stipulates to the matter being decided by the pro tem judge. The pro tem judge and counsel for defendant (both appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation form on their behalf.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' Motion to Compel Production of Medical Records from Dr. Friedlander is GRANTED. Dr. Friedlander is ordered to produce the documents responsive to the subpoena within two weeks of notice of this order. The Court finds that the records requested are relevant based upon plaintiff's allegations and discovery responses. Pursuant to CC Sec. 56.10, Dr. Friedlander cannot disclose the records absent an authorization or court order. Therefore, sanctions against Dr. Friedlander would be inappropriate. The request for sanctions is denied as to the parties and counsel as no statute or case law permits sanctions for a plaintiff's refusal to sign a medical authorization.

Order signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Peter Catalanotti; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
